source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure Activity Tracker data is encrypted at rest

input_row.source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure Activity Tracker data is encrypted at rest
input_row.description: Ensure Activity Tracker data is encrypted at rest.
input_row.rationale: LogDNA uses encrypted storage for all its rest data. Customers can elect to archive the Activity Tracker data to an IBM Cloud Object Storage (COS) bucket for long-term storage. By default, all objects that are stored in COS are encrypted by using randomly generated keys and an all-or-nothing-transform (AONT). Cloud Object Storage further supports additional key management scenarios to encrypt your data. This encryption model is called customer-managed encryption: 1. You can manage your keys manually by providing your own encryption keys - referred to as Server-Side Encryption with Customer-Provided Keys (SSE-C). 2. You can choose to use the integration capabilities with IBM Cloud® Key Management Services like IBM® Key Protect and Hyper Protect Crypto Services. Impact: Failure to adopt a customer-managed encryption model for audit data that is archived for long-term storage can breach infrastructure compliance where you are required to use your keys for encrypting data that you manage.
input_row.audit: 1. Ensure that you need long-term storage of your audit data. 2. Ensure that archiving is enabled on each of the Activity Tracker instances in your account. 3. If archiving is enabled, ensure that you have access to storage, for example, check that you have an instance of the IBM Cloud Object Storage service provisioned in your account. 4. Ensure that you have multiple COS buckets available to archive your data. Check the bucket configuration to verify that it meets your corporate and external market regulations. Consider using a bucket for each audit instance so that you can customize the bucket to meet security requirements. These requirements might be related to the type of data that is archived and to external regulations like EU-managed requirements, for example. 5. Ensure that the CIS control Ensure that archiving is enabled for audit events is met.
input_row.remediation: If you need long-term storage of your audit data and you do not have Cloud Object Storage configured to archive the data from your Activity Tracker instances, complete the following steps: 1. If the option to configure archiving is not allowed, upgrade the instance’s service plan. 2. If archiving is not enabled for an instance, review and define the archiving strategy. Then, configure archiving for an audit instance. 3. If you cannot find an instance of IBM Cloud Object Storage (COS) in your account, provision one. 4. If you have a bucket that does not have full control over the data encryption keys, configure one of the supported options. IBM® Cloud Object Storage provides several options to encrypt your data. Default Value: LogDNA uses encrypted storage for all its rest data. By default, all objects that are stored in COS are encrypted by using randomly generated keys and an all-or-nothing-transform (AONT). References: 1. IBM Cloud Object Storage: https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/cloud-object- storage?topic=cloud-object-storage-getting-started 2. IBM Cloud Object Storage managing encryption: https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/cloud-object-storage?topic=cloud-object-storage- encryption
input_row.impact: Failure to adopt a customer-managed encryption model for audit data that is archived for long-term storage can breach infrastructure compliance where you are required to use your keys for encrypting data that you manage.
input_row.references: 1. IBM Cloud Object Storage: https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/cloud-object- storage?topic=cloud-object-storage-getting-started 2. IBM Cloud Object Storage managing encryption: https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/cloud-object-storage?topic=cloud-object-storage- encryption

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

The control focuses on ensuring that Activity Tracker data, specifically when archived in IBM Cloud Object Storage (COS), is encrypted at rest. Given the extensive integration of IBM Cloud services and availability of APIs, the encryption state of objects in COS can be verified using IBM Cloud APIs and IBM Cloud CLI commands. Here's the rationale for an automated approach:

- **IBM Cloud APIs and CLI:** IBM Cloud provides APIs and CLI commands to interact with and manage Cloud Object Storage, including querying its encryption status.
- **Standard Service Property:** The requirement to ensure that data is encrypted at rest is a typical cloud service compliance check that can be verified programmatically.
- **Command Availability:** The IBM Cloud CLI has commands for checking encryption settings on Cloud Object Storage buckets.
- References and documentation indicate this is an object storage check, a feature typically exposed through programmatic methods.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_cos_bucket_encryption_at_rest_verified

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

1. **API Calls/CLI Commands:**
   - Use the IBM Cloud CLI command `ibmcloud cos bucket-encryption` to retrieve and check the encryption configuration for a specific COS bucket.
   - Execute `ibmcloud cos list-buckets` to enumerate all buckets available under the IBM Cloud account.

2. **Configuration and Verification:**
   - For each bucket, use `ibmcloud cos bucket-encryption --bucket <bucket-name>` to verify the presence of Server-Side Encryption.
   - Ensure that the encryption configuration is either default (AES-256) or configured with a customer-managed key (via Key Protect or Hyper Protect Crypto Services).

3. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - **Pass:** Every bucket used for Activity Tracker log storage must have an encryption configuration where ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration specifies either AES-256 or a customer-managed key is enabled.
   - **Fail:** If any bucket used for storing archived audit logs is found without encryption, or with a misconfigured encryption setting not conforming to customer-managed specifications.

This automated approach will ensure compliance by confirming through IBM Cloud APIs that all relevant buckets have the proper encryption settings in place, leveraging both default and customer-managed encryption capabilities.
